steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Let w = width (cm). The length is 35 cm greater than the width, so length = w + 35.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Area of a rectangle = length × width = (w + 35)(w) = w(w + 35).

choiceElimination:
• Choice A — $A=(w)(w+35)$: Width is w, length is w + 35; their product gives the correct area formula. ✓
  • Choice B — $A=(w)(35w)$: This would mean length = 35w (35 times the width), not 35 more than the width. ✗
  • Choice C — $A=(w)(\frac{w}{35})$: This makes length a fraction of the width, contradicting "35 cm greater." ✗
  • Choice D — $A=(w)(w-35)$: This subtracts 35 instead of adding it, reversing the relationship. ✗
That matches Choice A — $A=(w)(w+35)$.
